title: Genesis: Covenant Confirmed
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: covenant confirmed and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: covenant confirmed and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What sign did God require to mark all male members of Abraham's household under the Abrahamic covenant? Answer: circumcision.
acceptable_variants: cutting of the foreskin as a covenant sign

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The cutting of the male foreskin required of all Abraham's household as the physical sign of the Abrahamic covenant (Genesis 17)
A form of ancient Near Eastern covenant in which a superior grants land or privilege to a vassal unconditionally; scholars classify God's covenant with Abraham in this category
